summary: Crypto markets show mild consolidation with Bitcoin stable near $78k amid record high discussions, while commodities like gold surge to $4,700+ on sustained institutional long positioning and silver gains 2%. Forex and oil exhibit neutral-to-volatile sentiment with Brent ranging 90-125 amid supply concerns, and analysts highlight bullish macro hedges despite de-risking.

signals.name: Bitcoin
signals.reason: Expects Bitcoin and altcoins to pop this weekend with potential breakout higher.

signals.name: Altcoins
signals.reason: Altcoins to pop this weekend following Bitcoin.

signals.name: Ethereum
signals.reason: Down 1% trading around $2,300 as per recent market update.

signals.name: Solana
signals.reason: Down 3% trading around $84 with not much market action.

signals.name: Ripple
signals.reason: Down 2.8% trading at $138 amid flat crypto index.

signals.name: Gold
signals.reason: Trading at $4,712 with institutional net-long positioning stabilized at 26% of open interest, confirming structural hedging demand.

signals.name: Silver
signals.reason: Up 2.18% to $80 amid higher-beta proxy to gold with net-long bias trimmed but intact.

signals.name: Brent Crude Oil
signals.reason: Neutral-to-volatile Q2 range of 90-125 with key resistance at 110-115 amid SPR drawdowns capping momentum.

signals.name: EUR/USD
signals.reason: Neutral with moderate upward bias above 1.1600 in Q2 range 1.1450-1.2050 near 1.17 sideways structure.

signals.name: BNB
signals.reason: Down 2% trading around $610-620 amid subdued crypto market action.

signals.name: S&P 500
signals.reason: Bulls target 7,450 fueled by mega-cap tech initial May rally.
